title: Source pending: verified Quran Arabic text and English translation
note: Placeholder only. Replace with a verified Quran text source and named translator before publication.

title: Source pending: verified Bible passage and version
note: Placeholder only. Replace with the exact Bible version, publisher/source, and passage reference before publication.

title: Source pending: historical or academic context
note: Placeholder only. Replace with a specific academic source before making historical claims.

title: Citation needed
note: Use this marker only for draft material that must not be published until a real source is attached.

title: The Quran — Tanzil Uthmani text with Saheeh International translation (served via api.alquran.cloud)
note: Arabic text and translation fetched programmatically per verse; verify spot-checks against a printed mushaf and the published Saheeh International edition before final publication.

title: The Holy Bible, World English Bible (public domain)
note: Verse text fetched programmatically via bible-api.com; public-domain translation suitable for quotation.

title: The Character of Consciousness
author: David J. Chalmers
note: A major contemporary collection defending the irreducibility of consciousness and developing the hard problem.

title: The Study Quran
author: Seyyed Hossein Nasr and others (editors)
note: A translation and extensive commentary useful for tracing classical interpretations of Quranic terms for spirit and self.

title: Avicenna's De anima (Arabic Text): Being the Psychological Part of Kitab al-Shifa'
author: Fazlur Rahman (editor)
note: A primary scholarly edition for Avicenna's influential philosophical psychology; intended for advanced readers.

title: Arguing about Gods
author: Graham Oppy
note: A detailed philosophical critique of major theistic arguments, valuable for testing the case against a sophisticated non-theistic assessment.

title: The Existence of God
author: Richard Swinburne
note: A prominent cumulative-case defense of theism using explanatory reasoning.

title: The Quran and the Secular Mind: A Philosophy of Islam
author: Shabbir Akhtar
note: A sustained philosophical engagement between Quranic faith and secular objections.

title: Ethics Without God
author: Kai Nielsen
note: A secular philosophical defense of ethics independent of religious belief.

title: God and Morality: A Philosophical History
author: John E. Hare
note: A historical and philosophical study of how moral obligation has been related to God.

title: The Moral Landscape
author: Sam Harris
note: A widely discussed naturalistic proposal relating moral truth to the well-being of conscious creatures.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor E. Frankl
note: A classic psychiatric and existential reflection on meaning amid severe suffering; the work should not be treated as a specifically Islamic argument.

title: The Vision of Islam
author: Sachiko Murata and William C. Chittick
note: An integrated introduction to Islamic practice, faith, and spiritual excellence.

title: Islam's Quantum Question: Reconciling Muslim Tradition and Modern Science
author: Nidhal Guessoum
note: A critical assessment of the i'jaz 'ilmi movement by a Muslim astrophysicist, including a chapter-length evaluation of specific "scientific miracle" claims and their methodological weaknesses.

title: Science and Religion: A Very Short Introduction
author: Thomas Dixon
note: A concise historical and philosophical introduction that moves beyond a simple conflict narrative.

title: Where the Conflict Really Lies: Science, Religion, and Naturalism
author: Alvin Plantinga
note: A philosophical argument distinguishing science from metaphysical naturalism and examining areas of tension.

title: Islamic Theology and the Problem of Evil
author: Safaruk Chowdhury
publisher: The American University in Cairo Press
note: A study of major Islamic theological approaches to evil, including their accounts of value, freedom, and divine wisdom.

title: The Problem of Evil
author: Peter van Inwagen
note: A contemporary philosophical defense of theism addressing both global and local arguments from evil.

title: The Doors of the Sea: Where Was God in the Tsunami?
author: David Bentley Hart
note: A short Christian theological reflection that refuses to treat every tragedy as a directly intended divine lesson.

title: Revelation: From Metaphor to Analogy
author: Richard Swinburne
note: A philosophical account of why revelation might be expected and how its content could be assessed.

title: The Cambridge Companion to the Qur'an
author: Jane Dammen McAuliffe (editor)
note: A multi-author scholarly guide to the Quran's formation, themes, interpretation, and reception.

title: Muhammad: A Very Short Introduction
author: Jonathan A. C. Brown
note: A concise introduction to Muhammad in Islamic tradition and modern historical study.

title: The Qur'an: A Historical-Critical Introduction
author: Nicolai Sinai
note: An academic introduction to the Quran's historical setting, literary features, structure, and major scholarly debates.

title: The History of the Qur'anic Text: From Revelation to Compilation
author: Muhammad Mustafa al-A'zami
note: A traditional Muslim defense of Quranic transmission, useful to read alongside critical manuscript scholarship.

title: 1177 B.C.: The Year Civilization Collapsed
author: Eric Cline
note: Comprehensive scholarly analysis of Late Bronze Age collapse and its causes, relevant to understanding large-scale societal disruption patterns relevant to Quranic narratives about destroyed civilizations.

title: Discovering the City of Sodom
author: Steven Collins and Latayne C. Scott
note: Archaeological investigation of potential Sodom sites in the Dead Sea region, presenting evidence for catastrophic destruction patterns and site identification debates in the southern Levant.

title: Babylon: Mesopotamia and the Birth of Civilization
author: Paul Kriwaczek
note: Historical and archaeological overview of Babylon, including detailed discussion of the Etemenanki ziggurat and multilingual character of the city.

title: The Religion of the Nabataeans: A Conspectus
author: John F. Healey
note: Scholarly examination of Nabatean religious practices and culture in northern Arabia, providing archaeological and epigraphic context for understanding pre-Islamic Arabian societies.

title: The Canon of the New Testament: Its Origin, Development, and Significance
author: Bruce M. Metzger
note: A standard scholarly history of how the twenty-seven-book New Testament canon took shape.

title: The Canon of Scripture
author: F. F. Bruce
note: An evangelical Christian scholar's account of canon formation for both the Old and New Testaments.

title: The Biblical Canon: Its Origin, Transmission, and Authority
author: Lee Martin McDonald
note: A detailed survey of the debates and criteria behind canon formation.

title: The History of the Qur'anic Text from Revelation to Compilation: A Comparative Study with the Old and New Testaments
author: Muhammad Mustafa al-Azami
note: A Muslim scholar's comparative study of Quranic and biblical transmission.

title: The Resurrection of Jesus: History, Experience, Theology
author: Gerd Lüdemann
note: A historical-critical examination of resurrection and its interpretation in early Christianity; useful for understanding how Christians developed interpretations of Isaiah 53 and other passages.

title: Jesus and the Victory of God
author: N.T. Wright
note: A major work placing Jesus within Second Temple Judaism and messianic expectations; demonstrates how Christian interpretation adapted existing Jewish prophetic categories.

title: Studies in Jewish Myth and Jewish Messianism
author: Yehuda Liebes
note: Explores Jewish mystical messianic interpretation; illuminates how Jewish communities engaged creatively with prophetic texts across different periods.

title: Essays on Biblical Theology
author: Hartmut Gese
note: A comparative study of how Jewish and Christian traditions developed distinct hermeneutical methods; foundational for understanding interpretive diversity.

title: Jesus in the Qur'an
author: Geoffrey Parrinder
note: A classic study of how the Quran presents Jesus as Messiah and prophet, and of the theological grounds on which Islamic tradition diverges from Christian messianic and christological readings.

title: The Logic of God Incarnate
author: Thomas V. Morris
note: A rigorous analytic-philosophy defense of the logical coherence of the two-natures doctrine, including the two-minds model discussed in this article.

title: Christ in Christian Tradition: From the Apostolic Age to Chalcedon
author: Aloys Grillmeier
note: The standard scholarly history of how the doctrine of Christ's two natures developed through the early councils, including Nicaea, Ephesus, and Chalcedon.

title: The Christian God
author: Richard Swinburne
note: A leading analytic philosopher of religion's defense of the coherence of the Trinity and Incarnation, useful for readers who want the strongest contemporary philosophical case.

title: Al-Jawab al-Sahih li-man Baddala Din al-Masih (The Correct Answer to Those Who Have Corrupted the Religion of Christ)
author: Ibn Taymiyyah
note: A major classical Muslim theological engagement with Christian doctrines, including the Incarnation and Trinity, argued from within Islamic philosophical categories.

title: The Bible, the Qur'an and Science
author: Maurice Bucaille
note: The foundational and most widely circulated text of the modern "scientific miracles" (i'jaz) genre discussed throughout this article; hugely popular in Muslim apologetics, but its methodology has also drawn substantial scholarly critique from both Muslim and non-Muslim reviewers.

title: Tafsir Ibn Kathir (abridged English edition)
author: Ibn Kathir (trans. various)
note: The classical fourteenth-century commentary cited in this article for its reading of the sun/moon light distinction (10:5) and for the Day-of-Judgment context of Quran 27:88.

title: Ma'arif-ul-Qur'an
author: Mufti Muhammad Shafi (trans. Muhammad Hasan Askari and Muhammad Shamim, rev. Muhammad Taqi Usmani)
note: A widely used twentieth-century commentary cited in this article for its explicit caution against over-reading the diya'/nur distinction between the sun and moon as a claim about reflected versus intrinsic light.

title: On the Reliability of the Old Testament
author: Kenneth A. Kitchen
note: Eerdmans, 2003. A comprehensive examination of Old Testament texts against Egyptian, Mesopotamian, and other external sources, addressing chronological reconstruction and historical reliability of biblical narratives from Abraham through the monarchy.

title: Al-Tabari's History of the Prophets and Kings (Tarikh al-Rusul wa'l-Muluk)
author: Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari
note: A foundational Islamic historical work that synthesizes Quranic material, hadith, and historical reports to construct a detailed chronology from creation through the Islamic period; represents traditional Islamic historiography.

title: The Bible in History: How Writers Create a Past
author: Thomas L. Thompson
note: Pimlico, 1999 (published as 'The Mythic Past' in the US edition). A critical historical analysis examining how biblical writers constructed chronological and narrative frameworks; illustrates modern scholarly approaches to biblical historicity and chronology.

title: Narratives of Islamic Origins: The Beginnings of Islamic Historical Writing
author: Fred M. Donner
note: 1998. An examination of how early Islamic historians constructed their narratives and chronologies, including how Islamic sources related to and reinterpreted biblical and pre-Islamic Arabian materials.

title: Battlefields of the Prophet Muhammad: A Contribution to Muslim Military History
author: Muhammad Hamidullah
note: Historical study computing troop numbers and casualties across the Prophet's military campaigns from classical Arabic sources.

title: Did God Really Command Genocide? Coming to Terms with the Justice of God
author: Paul Copan and Matthew Flannagan
note: Book-length philosophical and theological defense of reading the herem texts as bounded ancient Near Eastern conquest rhetoric (Baker Books, 2014).

title: The Lost World of the Israelite Conquest: Covenant, Retribution, and the Fate of the Canaanites
author: John H. Walton and J. Harvey Walton
note: Argues that herem means removal from ordinary use rather than literal annihilation in every instance, using internal biblical evidence (IVP Academic, 2017).

title: Arguing the Just War in Islam
author: John Kelsay
note: Detailed examination of Islamic just-war theory and ethical principles governing warfare in classical Islamic jurisprudence and modern applications

title: Islam and Science
author: Muzaffar Iqbal
note: Historical and philosophical analysis of the relationship between Islamic civilization and scientific inquiry, examining the scriptural foundations and cultural conditions that shaped Islamic scientific tradition.

title: Science and Religion in Quest of Truth
author: John Polkinghorne
note: Theoretical physicist and theologian's careful examination of how science and religious belief address different questions, with analysis of common errors in concordism and the proper relationship between faith and empirical discovery. Yale University Press, 2011.

title: The Holy Qur'an and the Sciences of Nature
author: Mehdi Golshani
note: Iranian physicist-philosopher's exploration of how Quranic teachings relate to natural science, with emphasis on proper interpretation of scriptural language and avoidance of anachronistic readings of ancient texts.

title: God's Undertaker: Has Science Buried God?
author: John Lennox
note: Mathematician and philosopher of science's Christian-apologetics response to New Atheism, examining cosmological fine-tuning, the origin of the universe, and the limits of naturalistic explanation; addressed primarily to a Christian audience, with only passing comparative references to other traditions.

title: History of the Christian Church, Vol. 3: Nicene and Post-Nicene Christianity
author: Philip Schaff
note: A classical historical treatment covering the Council of Nicaea, providing detailed accounts of the proceedings, theological debates, and the biographies of key participants. Valuable for understanding the council from a Christian historical perspective.

title: Arius: Heresy and Tradition
author: Rowan Williams
note: A scholarly examination of Arius and Arianism that challenges some traditional characterizations. Williams presents the theological sophistication of Arian thought and explores why it had significant support in the early church.

title: Constantine and the Bishops: The Politics of Intolerance
author: H.A. Drake
note: Analyzes Constantine's role in church affairs, including his convening of Nicaea. Drake explores how Constantine used religious policy as a tool of imperial governance and the implications for church-state relations.

title: The Crucifixion and the Quran: A Study in the History of Muslim Thought
author: Todd Lawson
note: An Islamic scholar's examination of how Muslim thought, from the Quran through classical and modern exegesis, has understood the crucifixion of Jesus and related Christological claims. Provides Islamic perspective on the historical development of Christian doctrine including Nicene Christianity.

title: Christians, Muslims, and Jesus
author: Mona Siddiqui
note: A comparative theological study tracing Muslim and Christian views of Jesus from the Quran through medieval polemics to the present, including chapters on early debates over God's oneness and the formation of Christian doctrine—offering an Islamic-informed perspective to balance the largely Christian-historical sources above.

title: The Blind Watchmaker: Why the Evidence of Evolution Reveals a Universe Without Design
author: Richard Dawkins
note: Classic defense of non-teleological evolution and critique of design arguments; influential but polarizing within religious communities.

title: Nature Red in Tooth and Claw: Theism and the Problem of Animal Suffering
author: Michael J. Murray
note: Sophisticated philosophical analysis of how theism relates to evolutionary biology and the theodicy problem of natural suffering, particularly animal pain over evolutionary timescales.

title: Evolution and Islam's Quantum Question
author: Rana Dajani
publisher: Zygon: Journal of Religion and Science
note: Published in Zygon: Journal of Religion and Science 47:2 (2012); a contemporary Muslim biologist's perspective on reconciling evolutionary theory with Islamic theology. Zygon: Journal of Religion and Science, vol. 47, no. 2, pp. 343-353, DOI 10.1111/j.1467-9744.2012.01259.x

title: Dialogues Concerning Natural Religion
author: David Hume
note: 18th-century philosophical critique of design arguments; prescient anticipation of problems that evolutionary theory would later address.

title: The Crusades: A History
author: Jonathan Riley-Smith
note: Comprehensive historical account examining religious, political, and economic dimensions of the Crusades with attention to multiple perspectives

title: The Crusades Through Arab Eyes
author: Amin Maalouf
note: Provides Islamic and Arab perspective on Crusade-era conflicts, examining how Muslim societies experienced and responded to crusader invasions

title: Fighting for the Cross: Crusading to the Holy Land
author: Norman Housley
note: Examines crusading theology, its development in medieval Christianity, and how religious justifications for warfare were constructed and challenged

title: On the Trinity (De Trinitate)
author: Augustine of Hippo
note: The foundational work of Western Trinitarian theology. Augustine attempts to reconcile biblical passages about God's threeness and oneness through philosophical analysis and seeks human analogies for the Trinity.

title: Against the Arians
author: Athanasius of Alexandria
note: Athanasius's defense of homoousios (consubstantiality) against Arius. These works explain the theological stakes of the Arian controversy and Athanasius's arguments for Christ's full divinity.

title: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
author: John of Damascus
note: A systematic presentation of Christian Orthodox doctrine including Trinitarian theology, written in the 8th century. Reflects the Eastern Christian theological tradition shaped by the Councils.

title: The Bodies of God and the World of Ancient Israel
author: Benjamin D. Sommer
note: A scholarly study of theophany and divine presence across the Hebrew Bible, directly addressing the Exodus 33 tension between hearing and seeing God.

title: The Gospel According to John I–XII (The Anchor Bible)
author: Raymond E. Brown
note: A landmark critical commentary on John, with detailed discussion of John 1:18's textual variants and its theology of divine invisibility and revelation in Christ.

title: The Study Quran: A New Translation and Commentary
author: Seyyed Hossein Nasr (editor)
note: A major contemporary translation and commentary drawing on the classical tafsir tradition, with extended notes on Quran 6:103 and 7:143 and the theology of ru'yat Allah.

title: The Formative Period of Islamic Thought
author: W. Montgomery Watt
note: A scholarly history of early Islamic theological schools, including the Ash'ari–Mu'tazila debate over whether God will be seen in the Hereafter.

title: How Jesus Became God: The Exaltation of a Jewish Preacher from Galilee
author: Bart D. Ehrman
note: A critical historian traces how belief in Jesus’s divinity developed, including the rising christology from the earliest sources to John.

title: Lord Jesus Christ: Devotion to Jesus in Earliest Christianity
author: Larry W. Hurtado
note: The leading scholarly case that devotion to Jesus emerged early; included so readers can weigh the strongest counter-argument.

title: An Introduction to New Testament Christology
author: Raymond E. Brown
note: A careful treatment by a major Catholic scholar, candid about what the New Testament texts do and do not say about Jesus’s divinity.

title: Jesus the Jew: A Historian’s Reading of the Gospels
author: Geza Vermes
note: Reads Jesus within his first-century Jewish context, where titles like ‘son of God’ carried non-divine meanings.

title: Christology in the Making
author: James D. G. Dunn
note: Examines how the doctrine of the incarnation developed within the New Testament period itself.

title: Jesus, Prophet of Islam
author: Muhammad 'Ata ur-Rahim
note: An Islamic perspective on the history of beliefs about Jesus.

title: The Muslim Jesus: Sayings and Stories in Islamic Literature
author: Tarif Khalidi
note: A scholarly collection showing how deeply Jesus is loved and honored in the Islamic tradition.

title: How Jesus Became God
author: Bart D. Ehrman
note: A historical study by a non-Muslim scholar of how beliefs about Jesus' divinity developed in early Christianity.

title: Mere Christianity
author: C. S. Lewis
note: A classic presentation of mainstream Christian belief, useful for hearing the Christian position in its own voice.

title: The Islamic Jesus: How the King of the Jews Became a Prophet of the Muslims
author: Mustafa Akyol
note: A Muslim scholar's examination of Jesus' place in Islamic theology and how Islamic and Christian traditions diverge in understanding Jesus' identity and mission, engaging with historical and textual evidence from both scriptures.

title: Jesus and the Eyewitnesses: The Gospels as Eyewitness Testimony
author: Richard Bauckham
note: A Christian New Testament scholar's examination of the Gospel accounts and early Christian belief in Jesus' resurrection and exaltation, relevant to questions of Christological development.

title: The Fundamentals of Tawheed (Islamic Monotheism)
author: Bilal Philips
note: A foundational Islamic text explaining Tawhid as the core principle of Islam, clarifying what constitutes true monotheism and how it differs from polytheism and theological innovations.

title: The Trinity
author: Rowan Williams
note: A contemporary Christian theologian's examination of Trinitarian doctrine, exploring how the Trinity developed in Christian thought and how it relates to biblical witness, ancient creeds, and the nature of God.

title: The Historical Jesus: A Comprehensive Guide
author: Gerd Theissen and Annette Merz
note: A comprehensive scholarly overview of Jesus and early Christianity that integrates historical-critical methods with systematic theological reflection. Fortress Press, 1998. Accessible while maintaining academic rigor.

title: The Apostolic Fathers: Greek Texts and English Translations
author: Michael W. Holmes (translator and editor)
note: Authoritative bilingual edition of the Apostolic Fathers (Clement, Ignatius, Polycarp, Didache, and others) with Greek texts and English translations. Baker Academic, 3rd ed., 2007. Primary sources that illuminate early church life, theology, and responses to persecution.

title: The Cambridge Ancient History Vol. XI: The High Empire, A.D. 70-192
author: Edited by Alan K. Bowman, Peter Garnsey, Dominic Rathbone
note: Includes chapters on early Christianity within its broader Roman historical context, balancing Christian sources with external historical evidence.

title: An Introduction to the Science of the Hadith (Kitab Ma'rifat Anwa' 'Ilm al-Hadith)
author: Ibn al-Salah al-Shahrazuri (13th century), translated by Eerik Dickinson
note: A foundational classical work on hadith authentication methodology, part of the Great Books of Islamic Civilization series, explaining jarh wa ta'dil and isnad analysis in detail.

title: Studies in Hadith Methodology and Literature
author: Muhammad Mustafa al-Azami
note: A modern scholarly defense and exposition of Islamic hadith authentication methods, comparing them with historical and scientific standards, particularly useful for understanding the methodological rigor of early hadith critics.

title: Hadith: Origins and Developments
author: Edited by Harald Motzki
note: An edited collection of influential scholarly essays (including foundational contributions by Goldziher, Schacht, and Juynboll alongside responses) examining hadith criticism from both classical Islamic and modern Western academic perspectives, with attention to methodological issues in dating and provenance.

title: The New Testament Documents: Are They Reliable?
author: F.F. Bruce
note: A Christian scholar's exposition of Biblical manuscript evidence and textual criticism, useful for understanding the Christian documentary tradition in comparison with Islamic hadith transmission.

title: Hadith: Muhammad's Legacy in the Medieval and Modern World
author: Jonathan A.C. Brown
note: A comprehensive academic study of hadith criticism and methodology that engages both Islamic scholarly traditions and modern Western academic perspectives, including discussion of the Goldziher-Schacht thesis and Islamic scholars' responses. Essential for understanding contemporary debates over hadith reliability.

title: The Sanaa Palimpsest: The Transmission of the Qur'an in the First Centuries AH
author: Asma Hilali
note: An Oxford University Press academic study focused specifically on the Sanaa lower text and its significance.

title: Qur'ans of the Umayyads
author: François Déroche
note: A study by a leading specialist in Quranic codicology on how early Quran manuscripts were physically produced.

title: Textual Criticism and Qur'an Manuscripts
author: Keith E. Small
note: A comparative look at variant readings across early Quran manuscripts.

title: Misquoting Jesus
author: Bart D. Ehrman
note: An accessible introduction to New Testament textual criticism from a mainstream biblical scholar, useful for the comparison discussed in this article.

title: The Developing Human: Clinically Oriented Embryology, with Islamic Additions
author: Keith L. Moore, with Abdul Majeed Al-Zindani
note: Moore's standard embryology textbook, published in an edition with correlation studies relating its content to Quranic and hadith descriptions; presents the Muslim case for embryological precision in the Quran.

title: Medieval Islamic Medicine
author: Peter E. Pormann and Emilie Savage-Smith
note: Contextualizes Islamic medical knowledge, including theories of human generation, within pre-Islamic and contemporaneous Greco-Roman, Byzantine, and Persian medical traditions, demonstrating continuity rather than unique revelation.

title: Langman's Medical Embryology
author: T.W. Sadler
note: Standard modern embryology textbook providing detailed, stage-by-stage developmental biology from fertilization through birth.

title: The Genesis of Science: How the Christian Middle Ages Launched the Scientific Revolution
author: James Hannam
note: Explores how Christian theologians engaged with natural philosophy and science, contextualizing the relationship between scripture and scientific inquiry historically.

title: The Islamic Understanding of the Hereafter
author: Dr. Umar Al-Ashqar
note: Comprehensive classical Islamic scholarly treatment of eschatological beliefs, signs of the hour, and the Day of Judgment based on Quranic and hadith sources.

title: The Last Things: Biblical Eschatology
author: Geerhardus Vos
note: Systematic treatment of Christian end-times doctrine from biblical sources, examining the Second Coming, resurrection, and final judgment across New Testament texts.

title: Christ in Islam and Christianity: The Representation of Jesus in the Qur'an and the Classical Muslim Commentaries
author: Neal Robinson
publisher: State University of New York Press
note: Academic study of Quranic and classical Muslim interpretations of Jesus, including his return, crucifixion, miracles, and birth. ISBN 9780791405581

title: Revelation and its Interpretations
author: Grant Osborne
note: Scholarly analysis of Revelation's apocalyptic imagery and major interpretive frameworks for understanding Christian end-times theology.

title: Eve and Adam: Jewish, Christian, and Muslim Readings on Genesis and Gender
author: Kristen E. Kvam, Linda S. Schearing, and Valarie H. Ziegler (eds.)
note: An anthology of primary sources from all three traditions on Eve and the fall, letting readers compare the interpretive history side by side (Indiana University Press, 1999).

title: Women in the Qur’an, Traditions, and Interpretation
author: Barbara Freyer Stowasser
note: The standard academic study of how the Quran’s joint fall-narrative differs from later hadith and tafsir material on Eve (Oxford University Press, 1994).

title: Adam, Eve, and the Serpent
author: Elaine Pagels
note: A historian’s account of how early Christians, from Paul to Augustine, transformed the Genesis story and built doctrines of sin and sexuality on it (Random House, 1988).

title: God and the Rhetoric of Sexuality
author: Phyllis Trible
note: A close literary reading of Genesis 2-3 arguing that the text describes rather than prescribes male rule; influential in modern Christian exegesis of Genesis 3:16 (Fortress Press, 1978).

title: Verification Handbook
author: Craig Silverman, editor
note: A practical open handbook on verifying digital content and user-generated material.

title: News Literacy: Helping Students and Teachers Decode Fake News
author: Alan C. November
note: A practical introduction to evaluating online information; apply its methods alongside subject-specific expertise.

title: The Qur'an: A New Translation
author: M. A. S. Abdel Haleem
note: A widely used modern English translation; consult Arabic and recognised tafsir for detailed legal or theological interpretation.

title: The Ethics of War and Peace in Islam
author: Majid Khadduri
note: A foundational academic study of Islamic legal and ethical traditions; later scholarship should be consulted for critique and development.

title: Jerusalem: The Contested City
author: Menachem Klein
note: A scholarly political history of modern Jerusalem; use it for its documented argument rather than as a substitute for primary sources.

title: Jerusalem: A Biography
author: Simon Sebag Montefiore
note: A broad narrative history useful for orientation; readers should verify individual claims against specialist scholarship.

title: The Question of Palestine
author: United Nations
note: United Nations portal collecting documents and background material; primary institutional sources should be read with their authorship and mandate in mind.

title: The Palestine Question in Maps: 1878-2013
author: P. R. Kumaraswamy
note: A map-based historical reference; maps should be read with their dates, makers, and stated purposes.

title: Palestine and the Arab-Israeli Conflict: A History with Documents
author: Charles D. Smith
note: A widely assigned historical survey with documents; compare it with specialist works for particular periods.

title: Palestinian Identity: The Construction of Modern National Consciousness
author: Rashid Khalidi
note: A major scholarly study of the development of Palestinian national consciousness; read alongside other historians for differing emphases.

title: The Palestinians: From Peasants to Revolutionaries
author: Rosemary Sayigh
note: An influential social history that foregrounds Palestinian voices and displacement; its perspective should be identified when used.

title: Jerusalem in the Qur'an
author: Angelika Neuwirth
note: A scholarly discussion of Qur'anic and early Islamic approaches to Jerusalem; consult the full bibliographic context before relying on a single interpretation.

title: The History of al-Tabari, Volume VI: Muhammad at Mecca
author: al-Tabari, translated by W. Montgomery Watt and M. V. McDonald
publisher: State University of New York Press
note: An English translation of an important classical historical source; it records tradition and requires historical comparison rather than uncritical use.
